rationale: The subject property (orange dashed boundary, Humble TX) appears to be mid-construction or recently framed, with scattered lumber visible around the perimeter. The visible roof plane — a large, predominantly gray surface — fills much of the footprint when viewed from above, indicating a relatively moderate-to-shallow pitch. Cast shadows at the eave lines are present but not dramatically elongated relative to the building's plan footprint, ruling out steep pitches (≥8:12). The upper-left portion of the structure shows a darker, more conventionally pitched hip-roof section consistent with the surrounding neighborhood homes, which are typical suburban Houston construction generally ranging 5:12–7:12. The combination of moderate shadow length, the fact that a significant portion of the roof deck is visible (not foreshortened into a narrow ridge), and the regional norm for suburban Humble/Harris County construction (tile/shingle hip roofs at 5:12–6:12) points to approximately 6:12. Confidence is tempered by the active construction state of the property, which obscures some typical finishing cues.
